input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You have two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when you swap the `ResourceDictionary`:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when the dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes at runtime by replacing merged dictionaries:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(useDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

**2. Android restarts the Activity on `uiMode` changes.**
Add `UiMode` to `ConfigChanges` in your `MainActivity` so Android doesn't recreate the activity:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode   // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

You can then detect the system theme change and react to it:

```csharp
// In App.xaml.cs
Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    bool isDark = e.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark;
    // swap your dictionaries here
};
```

**TL;DR** — `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` for runtime updates; add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to prevent activity restarts.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and doesn't update when the resource dictionary changes. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android settings, the app restarts because Android recreates the activity on configuration changes by default. They need to handle the `uiMode` configuration change in their Android manifest.

Let me give a concise answer.
